整套首页
unit_16 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_16

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 279 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Sentence Equivalence
Although legislators on both sides of the issues have repeatedly ______ a desire to find a middle ground, until now no acceptable compromise has been achieved.Although legislators on both sides of the issues have repeatedly ______ a desire to find a middle ground, until now no acceptable compromise has been achieved.
答案：B, C　proclaimed; professed
middle groundcompromise
middle ground: standard unit headword | compromise: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage
panaceaobviaterecede
panacea: standard unit headword | obviate: standard unit headword | recede: standard unit headword
#3 TC SINGLE
Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.
答案：D　least potent
gravitydominate
gravity: standard unit headword | dominate: standard unit headword
#4 TC DOUBLE
Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　collegiality; personally likable
gravitategalvanize
gravitate: standard unit headword | galvanize: standard unit headword
#5 TC TRIPLE
The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated
dilutecurtail
dilute: standard unit headword | curtail: standard unit headword
#6 Sentence Equivalence
Although scientific progress leads to constant revision of ideas, one observation that has remained ______ over the years is that there are a lot of insects in the world: some 950,000 species have been identified.Although scientific progress leads to constant revision of ideas, one observation that has remained ______ over the years is that there are a lot of insects in the world: some 950,000 species have been identified.
答案：A, C　robust; strong
obscurerobust
obscure: standard unit headword | robust: standard unit headword
#7 Text Completion
Arguments based on mere possibilities (i)______ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii)______ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as “Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about” can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii)______ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.Arguments based on mere possibilities (i)______ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii)______ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as “Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about” can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii)______ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　epitomize; polarize; decisive
forestallempirical
forestall: standard unit headword | empirical: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
Though Blandford's manifesto envisions the victory of his political philosophy as __________, Dreyer denies that the manifesto is a deterministic document.Though Blandford's manifesto envisions the victory of his political philosophy as __________, Dreyer denies that the manifesto is a deterministic document.
答案：E　inevitable
provisionalimminent
provisional: standard unit headword | imminent: standard unit headword

Far from (i)______ the actions taken by the newspaper’s executives, Willem praised the executives’ resistance to corruption – yet he doubted that their policies were practical enough to warrant (ii)______ by other papers.Far from (i)______ the actions taken by the newspaper’s executives, Willem praised the executives’ resistance to corruption – yet he doubted that their policies were practical enough to warrant (ii)______ by other papers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　impugning; emulation

Blank 2
答案：B, F　abundance; rapacious depletion

答案：B　impede the growth of

答案：C　measured

Blank 2

#17 SE
In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.
答案：B, D　haphazard; arbitrary

Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　constrain; vitiate
externalcompromise
external: standard unit headword | compromise: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　a rigorous; a faithful

Blank 2

Blank 2
Blank 3

decided

答案：C　predominant

Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) .As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) increased. #28 Sentence Equivalence
Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the ______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the ______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous
overarching
overarching: standard unit headword

Between 1975 and 2005 water use in the United States declined just as the population rose, showing the old assumption that total water consumption is positively correlated with population growth to be _______.Between 1975 and 2005 water use in the United States declined just as the population rose, showing the old assumption that total water consumption is positively correlated with population growth to be _______.
答案：B, C　specious; fallacious
fallacious
fallacious: standard unit headword

答案：D　flummery
diametrically

Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　monster; prickly; sympathetic
anything but
anything but: standard unit headword

#33 TC DOUBLE
Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　swagger; pugnacious
chic
chic: standard unit headword

答案：E　maudlin

答案：A, E　feigned; false

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 3

Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)______ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)______ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)______ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)______ by the frogs.

Blank 2
Blank 3

#50 Text Completion
Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay
memento
memento: standard unit headword
#51 Sentence Equivalence
The political upheaval caught most people by surprise: despite the ______ warning of some commentators, it had never seemed that imminent.The political upheaval caught most people by surprise: despite the ______ warning of some commentators, it had never seemed that imminent.
答案：B, C　prescient; prophetic
sternimminent
stern: standard unit headword | imminent: standard unit headword
#52 Text Completion
The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)______, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)______, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly
unwitting
unwitting: standard unit headword
#53 Text Completion
Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is ______.Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is ______.
答案：B　undiminished
bristle
bristle: standard unit headword
#54 Text Completion
Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)______ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)______ . People reasonably (iii)______ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)______ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)______ . People reasonably (iii)______ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　surfeit; virtue; forgo

答案：C　numinous

These facts necessitate that policies related to greenhouse gases differ from policies related to various pollutants whose effects are (iii)______ and often temporary.

This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)______ reception that academics—motivated partly by (iii)______ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards—generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author’s choice of format, which is the (i)______ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. Blank 1
Blank 2

For example, the (ii) ____________ Maxwell's equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.Many of the major developments in fundamental physics of the past century arose from (i) ____________ existing ideas. For example, the (ii) ____________ Maxwell's equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　overcoming contradictions between; incompatibility of

The thin glass at the end of their tails is (ii) __________, however-if you break it, the fracture will propagate through the drop at 2,000 miles per hour.Capable of withstanding repeated hammer blows, the heads of the tadpole-shaped glass droplets known as Prince Rupert's drops are highly (i) __________. The thin glass at the end of their tails is (ii) __________, however-if you break it, the fracture will propagate through the drop at 2,000 miles per hour.

Blank 2

#101 SE
The geometry student found the proposition she was supposed to demonstrate to be so as to obviate demonstration: since opposite angles were self-evidently equal, there was no need for a proof.The geometry student found the proposition she was supposed to demonstrate to be so as to obviate demonstration: since opposite angles were self-evidently equal, there was no need for a proof.

答案：C, E　obviate; displace

#103 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
conservation
conservation: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
Genetic diversity is the raw material of evolution, including the domestication of plants, yet the domestication process typically __________ diversity because the first domesticates are derived from a very small sample of the individual plants.Genetic diversity is the raw material of evolution, including the domestication of plants, yet the domestication process typically __________ diversity because the first domesticates are derived from a very small sample of the individual plants.
答案：C　involves a loss of
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword

Humans' earliest interactions with fire were undoubtedly :prehistoric hunters may have exploited naturally occurring fire to help corral prey, for example.Humans' earliest interactions with fire were undoubtedly :prehistoric hunters may have exploited naturally occurring fire to help corral prey, for example.
答案：B, D　opportunistic; improvisational

exploit: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　legitimate within; impede

Climate science journals are often too hermetic for even the most (i)_______ layperson. The gravity of the issues discussed requires that we (ii)_______ such (iii)_______: we need publications that engage the broader public in discussions of how we interact with the natural world.Climate science journals are often too hermetic for even the most (i)_______ layperson. The gravity of the issues discussed requires that we (ii)_______ such (iii)_______: we need publications that engage the broader public in discussions of how we interact with the natural world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

#108 TC SINGLE

答案：A　tempers
exploit
exploit: standard unit headword
#109 Sentence Equivalence
Many theorists believe that measures to prevent industrial pollution necessarily increase production costs, but several recent reports document innovations that ______ environmental harm while also delivering economic benefits.Many theorists believe that measures to prevent industrial pollution necessarily increase production costs, but several recent reports document innovations that ______ environmental harm while also delivering economic benefits.
答案：D, E　mitigate; curtail
curtail
curtail: standard unit headword
#110 TC DOUBLE
Many of the major developments in fundamental physics of the past century arose from (i) existing ideas. For example, the (ii) Maxwell' s equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.Many of the major developments in fundamental physics of the past century arose from (i) existing ideas. For example, the (ii) Maxwell' s equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　overcoming contradictions between; incompatibility of
gravity
gravity: standard unit headword
#111 SE
Actors may have swooned over him, but producers, for whom the director lost vast amounts of money during his fifty-year filmmaking career, were less .Actors may have swooned over him, but producers, for whom the director lost vast amounts of money during his fifty-year filmmaking career, were less .
答案：B, E　enchanted; enthralled

producer: standard unit headword

An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.
答案：A, B　abound; proliferate
abound
abound: standard unit headword

Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) _________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of \ her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) _________ Was not (iii) _________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) _________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of \ her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) _________ Was not (iii) _________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　noted; approbation; unqualified
impugn
impugn: standard unit headword

Although preserved dinosaur tracks are themselves inherently __________, they can provide information about how the creatures moved, serving as evidence of a dynamism the tracks themselves do not possess.Although preserved dinosaur tracks are themselves inherently __________, they can provide information about how the creatures moved, serving as evidence of a dynamism the tracks themselves do not possess.
答案：C　static
fragile
fragile: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　misleading; differences

答案：C, D　proliferate; abound

abound: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　reconciliation; rapprochement

For example, more people might decide to drive alone instead of using public transportation. Historically, transportation and communications have been (ii) __________ to each other--both of them increasing concurrently--rather than (iii) ___________ for each other.If telecommuting ever did reduce traffic congestion noticeably , the excess capacity on the highways would almost certainly be quickly (i) ___________ by changes in current travel patterns. Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C, D　antagonism; hostility

Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. diametrically: standard unit headword
#161 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#162 Text Completion
Making loans and fighting poverty are normally two of the least glamorous pursuits around, but remarkably enough put the two together, and you have an economic innovation that has become not just (i)______ but downright (ii)______.Making loans and fighting poverty are normally two of the least glamorous pursuits around, but remarkably enough put the two together, and you have an economic innovation that has become not just (i)______ but downright (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　popular; chic
chic
chic: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
With its immense biomass of top predators, the Ross Sea is (i) __________ compared to other modern ocean ecosystems. Its high proportion of top predators (ii) __________ the standard textbook description of an ecological pyramid, in which primary producers-phytoplankton in ocean ecosystems-drive the system and make up most of the biomass.With its immense biomass of top predators, the Ross Sea is (i) __________ compared to other modern ocean ecosystems. Its high proportion of top predators (ii) __________ the standard textbook description of an ecological pyramid, in which primary producers-phytoplankton in ocean ecosystems-drive the system and make up most of the biomass.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　anomalous; inverts
producerrobust
producer: standard unit headword | robust: standard unit headword
#164 Sentence Equivalence
One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious
fabricate
fabricate: standard unit headword
#165 SE
In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly ________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly ________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.
答案：B, E　static; unvarying
fragile
fragile: standard unit headword
#166 Text Completion
In the early twentieth century, rural women in the United States could feel caught between, on one hand, national myths upholding a "natural" lifestyle that by definition seemed to eschew science and technology, and, on the other hand, a desire to (i)______ the new, modern standards of mechanized household practice that (ii)______ their (iii)______ by aligning them more and more with city women and modern life generally.In the early twentieth century, rural women in the United States could feel caught between, on one hand, national myths upholding a "natural" lifestyle that by definition seemed to eschew science and technology, and, on the other hand, a desire to (i)______ the new, modern standards of mechanized household practice that (ii)______ their (iii)______ by aligning them more and more with city women and modern life generally.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　adopt; threatened to compromise _; distinctiveness
compromise
compromise: standard unit headword
#167 Sentence Equivalence
Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only ______ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only ______ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.
答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious
consume
consume: standard unit headword
#168 SE
The impact of the film on people’s perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to _______ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.The impact of the film on people’s perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to _______ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.
答案：A, D　trump; supplant
exploit
exploit: standard unit headword
#169 Text Completion
What once seemed a quixotic vision—the “Subway to the Sea,” connecting Union Station in downtown Los Angeles to the Pacific Ocean in Santa Monica—no longer seems quite so ______.What once seemed a quixotic vision—the “Subway to the Sea,” connecting Union Station in downtown Los Angeles to the Pacific Ocean in Santa Monica—no longer seems quite so ______.
答案：A　impracticable
impracticable
impracticable: standard unit headword
#170 Sentence Equivalence
If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.
答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude
forestall
forestall: standard unit headword
#171 SE
__________ the idea that attention is a limited resource, scientists have repeatedly observed that drivers using mobile phones are slower to react and more apt to miss important details than are drivers focused solely on the road.__________ the idea that attention is a limited resource, scientists have repeatedly observed that drivers using mobile phones are slower to react and more apt to miss important details than are drivers focused solely on the road.
答案：B, D　Bolstering; Buttressing
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#172 Text Completion
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces
counterforce
counterforce: standard unit headword
#173 Sentence Equivalence
The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would ______ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would ______ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.
答案：A, B　combine; meld
overarching
overarching: standard unit headword
#174 SE
Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.
答案：B, D　pliant; malleable
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#175 Sentence Equivalence
The book brings together many valuable reports on conservation projects, but with less variety than might have been wished: nearly half the contributors are from the same state, and consequently, the case studies are similarly ______ geographically.The book brings together many valuable reports on conservation projects, but with less variety than might have been wished: nearly half the contributors are from the same state, and consequently, the case studies are similarly ______ geographically.
答案：D, F　circumscribed; restricted
conservation
conservation: standard unit headword
#176 Text Completion
In the 1980s, many historians sounded urgent calls for (i)______ in American historical writing, as longer and longer monographs on smaller and smaller subjects were being written—dazzling studies, but pieces of a puzzle no one was putting together. This scholarship was not illuminating the central themes of history but (ii)______ them.In the 1980s, many historians sounded urgent calls for (i)______ in American historical writing, as longer and longer monographs on smaller and smaller subjects were being written—dazzling studies, but pieces of a puzzle no one was putting together. This scholarship was not illuminating the central themes of history but (ii)______ them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　synthesis; obscuring
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#177 SE
Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.
答案：B, F　challenged; flouted
impede
impede: standard unit headword
#178 Sentence Equivalence
Changes made to ecosystem in order to achieve a goal, such as food production or flood control, often ______ significant unforeseen trade-offs between other important products and service the ecosystems provide.Changes made to ecosystem in order to achieve a goal, such as food production or flood control, often ______ significant unforeseen trade-offs between other important products and service the ecosystems provide.
答案：E, F　yield; engender
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#179 Text Completion
Though humanitarian emergencies are frequent features of television news, such exposure seldom ______ the public, which rather seems resigned to a sense of impotency.Though humanitarian emergencies are frequent features of television news, such exposure seldom ______ the public, which rather seems resigned to a sense of impotency.
答案：D　galvanizes
galvanize
galvanize: standard unit headword
#180 SE
Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.
答案：C, D　sterile; unfruitful
fallacious
fallacious: standard unit headword
#181 Sentence Equivalence
The astronomer admits that his interpretation of so-called Population Ⅲ stars is ______ at present since no one has yet done any real calculations to see if it holds up under closer scrutiny.The astronomer admits that his interpretation of so-called Population Ⅲ stars is ______ at present since no one has yet done any real calculations to see if it holds up under closer scrutiny.
答案：B, D　speculative; conjectural
fabricate
fabricate: standard unit headword
#182 Text Completion
In the search for truth, the knowledge gained by scientists consists of approximations with varying degrees of certainty. Such (i)______ truth can be highly (ii)______, as the rapid and relatively steady progress of medical knowledge well exemplifies.In the search for truth, the knowledge gained by scientists consists of approximations with varying degrees of certainty. Such (i)______ truth can be highly (ii)______, as the rapid and relatively steady progress of medical knowledge well exemplifies.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　provisional; useful
provisional
provisional: standard unit headword
#183 SE
Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.
答案：B, D　abjure; repudiate
obviate
obviate: standard unit headword
#184 Sentence Equivalence
Although extreme and singular events occur in a black hole, its event horizon boundary is ______ space travelers; crossing it would be subject to the same physical laws that apply anywhere else.Although extreme and singular events occur in a black hole, its event horizon boundary is ______ space travelers; crossing it would be subject to the same physical laws that apply anywhere else.
答案：C, F　unremarkable; commonplace
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#185 SE
There's ___________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.There's ___________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.
答案：B, D　a splendor; a sumptuousness

#186 Text Completion
Throughout much of the twentieth century, common scientific sense seemed to dictate that animals could not make a choice based on rational or aesthetic criteria. Such choices were (i)______ the mental capacity of humans. Scientists who (ii)______ this animal-human cognitive division were often accused of anthropomorphism.Throughout much of the twentieth century, common scientific sense seemed to dictate that animals could not make a choice based on rational or aesthetic criteria. Such choices were (i)______ the mental capacity of humans. Scientists who (ii)______ this animal-human cognitive division were often accused of anthropomorphism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　reserved for; transgressed

reserved: standard unit headword

答案：D, E　belie; mask

obviate: standard unit headword
#188 Sentence Equivalence
Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.
答案：D, E　solidarity; camaraderie
hierarchy
hierarchy: standard unit headword
#189 Text Completion
His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i)______ . He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i)______ . He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　forbearance; madcap; reserved
reserved
reserved: standard unit headword
#190 Sentence Equivalence
Although initially it may be difficult to discern the essay’s message of peace and conciliation, a close reading reveals its essentially ______ nature.Although initially it may be difficult to discern the essay’s message of peace and conciliation, a close reading reveals its essentially ______ nature.
答案：A, F　pacific; dovish
austere
austere: standard unit headword

The councilman was a highly respected, even ______ member of society, so when he was accused of fraud, people were (i) .The councilman was a highly respected, even ______ member of society, so when he was accused of fraud, people were (i) .
答案：A, D　venerated; stunned

obscure: standard unit headword

Proponents of international regulation of environmental issues have always struggled against scientific uncertainty and economic hostility, two obstacles which, from a political standpoint, often have been closely related, as economic hostility toward environmental regulation for economic reasons have (i)______ the considerable uncertainty underlying most environmental challenges to (ii)______ of environmental regulation.Proponents of international regulation of environmental issues have always struggled against scientific uncertainty and economic hostility, two obstacles which, from a political standpoint, often have been closely related, as economic hostility toward environmental regulation for economic reasons have (i)______ the considerable uncertainty underlying most environmental challenges to (ii)______ of environmental regulation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　exploited; downplay the legitimacy
exploit
exploit: standard unit headword

There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is ______ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is ______ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.
答案：D, F　awkward; ungainly

答案：D, E　civility; comity

But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i)______ the (ii)______ of forest.Boreal forest is at the southern boundary of the moss-dominated tundra, which remains characteristically treeless because its spongy surface retains water that cannot drain away through the underlying permafrost. But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i)______ the (ii)______ of forest.

Blank 2

答案：A, D　a naïve; an unsophisticated

#198 Sentence Equivalence
Motorcycle riders in London have discovered an ingenious, albeit ______ way off parking illegally without getting a ticket.Motorcycle riders in London have discovered an ingenious, albeit ______ way off parking illegally without getting a ticket.
答案：B, D　unscrupulous; dishonest
impractical

#199 Text Completion
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned
impugn
impugn: standard unit headword
#200 Sentence Equivalence
The political upheaval caught most people by surprise: despite the ______ warnings of some commentators, it had never seemed that imminent.The political upheaval caught most people by surprise: despite the ______ warnings of some commentators, it had never seemed that imminent.
答案：B, C　prescient; prophetic
sternimminent
stern: standard unit headword | imminent: standard unit headword
#201 Text Completion
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
dominate
dominate: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：E, F　mitigated; abated

答案：C, D　undermined; impaired

When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

Blank 3

#212 Text Completion
It was fine to be (i)______ in the old days when papers were still not losing readers and ad revenue was not tough to come by, but many editors today are uneasy about bludgeoning their readers with the inherently (ii)______ work of cartoonists.It was fine to be (i)______ in the old days when papers were still not losing readers and ad revenue was not tough to come by, but many editors today are uneasy about bludgeoning their readers with the inherently (ii)______ work of cartoonists.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　opinionated; unbalanced
opinionated

#213 Text Completion
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
fragile
fragile: standard unit headword

Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii)______ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.

Blank 2
答案：B, E　kill; consistent with

答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic

Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.

Blank 3

Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate

Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

#231 Text Completion
Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　supplies; strong bargaining positions

Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. By thus (i)______ films, archives would not only serve as repositories, but would provide (ii)______ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　interpreting; education

答案：E　plastic

#255 Text Completion
Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to

#258 Text Completion
One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern

The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)______ , but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)______ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)______ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)______ , but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)______ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)______ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.
Blank 1

答案：C, D, I　humanly audible; attenuate; astonished by

#267 Text Completion
The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift
